skill.name: report-writing
skill.description: Create structured completion reports that document changes, impact, validation results, and follow-up work.
skill.summary: Guides structured task completion reports with change history, impact analysis, validation evidence, and follow-up actions.
skill.author: Doyajin174

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported Ruby backtick execution, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found these are Markdown fences, examples, and text in SKILL.md. The only real external command indicator is benign documentation for git status and git diff, used to gather changed-file context for a report.

low_findings.title: Benign Git Command Examples
low_findings.description: SKILL.md includes git status --porcelain and git diff --name-only HEAD as documentation examples for collecting changed-file context. These commands are read-only and locally scoped, but they are still external commands if a host agent chooses to run them.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are directly visible in a bash example and have clear report-writing intent. They do not execute untrusted input, alter files, access secrets, or contact a network endpoint.

low_findings.title: Static Backtick Execution Alerts Are Markdown Formatting
low_findings.description: Most external command alerts point to Markdown code fences, inline filenames, table examples, and nested code block delimiters. No Ruby source file or executable backtick shell invocation was found in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited areas are Markdown examples and tables, not Ruby code. The skill contains only SKILL.md and declares Read, Glob, Grep, and Write tools.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alert Is Metadata Text
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm alert at line 3 is a false positive. The line is a Korean description of the report filename format and contains no hashing, encryption, or cryptographic API usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is frontmatter description text. No evidence of cryptographic implementation or weak algorithm use exists in the scanned file.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Alert Is Mermaid Diagram Syntax
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance alert at line 142 is a false positive. The line starts a Mermaid diagram example and does not gather system, host, user, or network information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding lines define a static Mermaid graph example. No reconnaissance command or system information access is present.

content.value_statement: Teams lose context when completed work is not documented clearly. This skill provides a repeatable report format for changes, impact, validation, and follow-up actions.
actual_capabilities: Defines a required report filename pattern under docs/reports.
Provides a complete Markdown report structure for completed work.
Documents changed files, change types, and detailed before-and-after context.
Adds impact analysis sections for direct effects, indirect effects, and unchanged items.
Includes validation results and follow-up task checklist sections.
Suggests related documentation skills for changelogs, decision records, and planning.
limitations: It does not generate reports automatically without project context from the user or agent.
It does not verify command output unless the host agent runs validation separately.
It does not enforce file naming outside the generated guidance.
It is optimized for Markdown reports, not slide decks or formal audit packets.

use_cases.title: Engineering Change Reports
use_cases.description: Document code changes, file updates, validation commands, and technical follow-up work after an implementation task.

use_cases.title: Migration Completion Notes
use_cases.description: Record what changed during a migration, which paths or systems were affected, and what remains to verify.

use_cases.title: Project Handoff Documentation
use_cases.description: Create concise handoff reports that explain decisions, impact, status, and pending actions for stakeholders.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Report
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner report after a small task
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the report-writing skill to create a completion report for my recent task. Include changed files, summary, validation results, and follow-up items.

prompt_templates.title: Document a Bug Fix
prompt_templates.scenario: Structured report for a resolved defect
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a report for this bug fix. Explain the root cause, changed files, impact, verification steps, and any remaining risk.

prompt_templates.title: Summarize a Migration
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the report format to document this migration. Include before-and-after structure, affected areas, preserved behavior, validation evidence, and next steps.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a Handoff Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a stakeholder-ready completion report. Group changes by type, explain direct and indirect impact, include verification evidence, and list follow-up decisions.

output_examples.input: A developer completed a folder rename and needs a durable record.
output_examples.output: A dated Markdown report with a change overview, renamed paths, before-and-after structure, validation results, and follow-up checklist.

output_examples.input: A team finished a security-related documentation update.
output_examples.output: A completion report that categorizes the change as security work, lists modified files, explains impact, and records verification steps.

output_examples.input: A project lead needs a handoff after a refactor.
output_examples.output: A concise report covering the reason for the refactor, affected modules, unchanged behavior, test evidence, and remaining tasks.

best_practices: Use the required date-based filename so reports remain sortable and easy to find.
Include actual validation commands and results instead of generic success statements.
Record why important items were not changed when they are relevant to impact analysis.
anti_patterns: Do not omit changed files when the report is meant to support future troubleshooting.
Do not claim verification was completed without evidence from tests, checks, or review.
Do not use the template for unrelated notes that lack changes, impact, or follow-up work.

faq.question: What does this skill help me create?
faq.answer: It helps create structured Markdown reports after work is completed.

faq.question: Where should reports be saved?
faq.answer: The skill specifies the docs/reports/YYYY-MM-DD-title-report.md filename pattern.

faq.question: Does it run validation commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides a place to record validation commands and results.

faq.question: Can it document non-code work?
faq.answer: Yes, if the work has clear changes, impact, status, and follow-up items.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is the skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. Review found only benign Markdown examples and read-only Git command guidance.
